description_long: This flower features a vibrant display of overlapping petals, predominantly coral-red with a distinct yellow center radiating outward. The petals are broad, rounded, and arranged in a radial symmetry, creating a full, layered appearance. The central part of the flower is slightly recessed, with a small, dark green to yellowish core. The underside reveals a green, conical calyx and a sturdy, elongated stem, with the calyx extending into pointed sepals. The flower's surface appears soft and delicate, with subtle veining visible on the petals. No distinct leaves are attached directly to the flower, but the green calyx and stem provide a natural contrast to the vivid petals.
description: A coral-red flower with a yellow center, broad overlapping petals, and a green stem and calyx. The petals are arranged radially, giving the flower a full, symmetrical look.
description_view_0: The flower is seen from above, showcasing its radial symmetry, coral-red petals, and yellow center.
description_view_1: A side view highlights the green stem and calyx, with the petals curving outward and downward.
description_view_2: The underside of the flower is visible, showing the green calyx and the base of the petals.
description_view_3: Another side view, emphasizing the elongated green stem and the layered arrangement of the petals.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a radial, whorled configuration with significant overlapping and layering, forming a nearly circular, symmetrical flower head.
